source_title: 02 Building Environments - The Basics/02 What are PBR Materials

evidences.content: 好了，让我们来谈谈材料，什么是 PBR 或基于物理的渲染表面，然后 All right so let's talk about materials what a PBR or physically based rendering surface is and then 我们将讨论主材料和材料实例，它们是材料的设置方式 we'll touch on master materials and material instances which are the way materials are set up 在虚幻中。那么什么是基于物理的材质呢？PBR 只是一个系统，在这个系统中，我们有 inside of Unreal. So what are physically based materials? So PBR is just a system where we have 图像通道定义了现实世界中物体对光线的反应。我们希望创建 image channels that define how objects react to light in the real world. We want to create 这样，我们就可以在场景中添加灯光，旋转我们的灯光 everything in a photorealistic way so that we can add lights into our scene, rotate our lights around 并让一切都以逼真的方式动态反应。因此，每个 PBR 材质 and have everything react dynamically in a photorealistic way. So every single PBR material 由基色、粗糙度和法线贴图组成。让我们来谈谈它们是什么。基色是 consists of base color, roughness and normal maps. So let's talk about what that is. So base color is 听起来像什么？这是模型的原始颜色，就像我们在这里看到的一样。这也可以是 what it sounds like. It's the raw color of the model like we can see here. This can also be 被称为反照率，如果你把任何物体的反光和亮度去掉 known as the albedo and if you take any object and you got rid of its reflection and its shininess 这就是你会看到的底色。你不需要任何阴影，你需要的是纯粹的底色。 this is what you would see underneath. You don't want any shadows you want the pure base color that 你对物体的印象。如果它是一个苹果，那就会是一张非常红的地图。因此，预览 you would have of the object. If it was an apple it'd be a very red map. So the best way to preview 这是从点亮模式进入非点亮模式，这基本上会移除所有照明 this is to go from lit mode into unlit mode and this will basically remove all of the lighting 数据，并显示场景中所有物体的基色。因此，如果我们查看反照率 data and reveal the base color of all of the objects in our scene. So if we go to the albedo 如果您想修改灰色区域的任何内容，只需点击小复选框即可。通过 controls here if you want to modify anything that's grayed out just click on the little checkbox. By 单击并拖动，我们就能清楚地看到这种效果对我们的对象产生了什么影响。 clicking and dragging we can see exactly what this effect does on our object and now if we go to lit 我们将看到这种模式所产生的影响。这就是反照率，但粗糙度与 mode we'll see the sort of effect that has. So that's albedo but roughness has to do with the 物体的反射。让我们看看右边的铬合金球。我们有 reflection of the object. So let's take a look at our chrome ball here on the right. So we have 粗糙度和金属选择。因此，并非每个物体都需要金属质感，但每个物体 roughness and metallic options. So not every object needs to be metallic but every object 将有一个粗糙度通道，粗糙度指的是物体反射的粗糙程度。因此 will have a roughness channel and roughness refers to how rough the reflection of our object is. So 如果我们将其设置为零，则表示粗糙度为零。如果我们点击并拖动这些，然后查看 if we set it to zero it means it has zero roughness. If we just click and drag these and we look at 当我们的铬球粗糙度为零时，它会变成纯反射，我们甚至可以看到 our chrome ball when we have zero roughness it turns into a pure reflection and we can even see 我们的雕像在铬合金球的倒影中，如果我们一直走到其中一个，你会看到 our statue in the reflection of our chrome ball here and if we go all the way to one you'll see 我们就会开始失去这种能见度，情况就会变得非常非常糟糕。具体来说 that we start to lose that visibility and it starts to become very very rough. Now specifically 粗糙度指的是什么？ what roughness is referring to if you imagine the surface at almost an atomic level how rough and 锯齿状的表面即使在分子水平上，也会有这些微小的凹凸。 jagged that surface would be. There's all of these micro bumps even down at that molecular level and 这就是导致任何物体都有很多粗糙度的原因。而你想象一个铬球或 that's what causes any object to have a lot of roughness. Whereas you imagine a chrome ball or 更好的办法是，把水想象成零粗糙度，或者想象成一面镜子，那表面是多么光滑啊 better yet even just think of water as having zero roughness or a mirror how smooth that surface 这是因为没有任何微观细节来打破这种反射。所以我会 would have to be and that's because there's no micro detail breaking up that reflection. So I'll 把它调回 0.2，你就能看到这种效果，这也是在真实世界中的一大好处。 set this back to 0.2 and you can see that effect that's one of the great parts of being in a real 时间渲染引擎，你就可以开始看到它的具体外观，甚至可以旋转到 time render engine is you can start to see exactly how that looks and you can even rotate around to 看看这对我们的物体有什么影响。 see what effect that has on our object and you can even see the reflection of the clouds in our 表面。然后是正常通道，正常通道只是我们添加 surface here. And then we have the normal channel and the normal channel is just a way for us to add 在不添加额外多边形或三角形的情况下，为我们的对象添加更多细节。因此，如果我转到 additional detail to our object without adding additional polygons or triangles. So if I go to 在这里，我们将看到这个物体的细节，您可能会希望看到这个线框 our wireframe here we'll see exactly how detailed this object is and you might expect this wireframe 有更多的细节，因为当我们近距离观察时，我们会看到所有这些细节，而它会对这些细节做出反应 to have a lot more details because when we look up close we have all of this detail and it reacts to 但实际上，这些光线来自法线贴图。因此，如果我设置 light in the way that we would expect but this is actually coming from the normal map. So if I set 你会发现我们的表面的细节少了很多，这是因为它更容易 this to zero you'll see that our surface has a lot less detail and this is because it's easier 在法线贴图中按像素处理这些信息，而不是创建几何图形 to process this information at a per pixel level in the normal map as opposed to creating geometry 相当于每一个像素。因此，如果我们看一下这里的法线贴图图像，我们可以看到所有的 for the equivalent of every single pixel. So if we look at our normal map image here we can see all 即使在这种微观层面上也能看到细节，而且您的图像分辨率更高 of the detail even at this micro level and the higher resolution that you have this image at 屏幕上的分辨率就越高。而我们的法线贴图并不是你创建的 the more resolution will get on screen. And our normal map is not something that you would create 在 Photoshop 中手工绘制的数据，最好由计算机来创建，通常是在烘焙时绘制下来 by hand in Photoshop this is data that is best created by a computer typically when baking down 高分辨率的雕刻或照片扫描图像，然后使用纹理转换器将其浓缩成纹理。 high resolution sculpt or photo scans and condensing it down into a texture using an 自动化流程。因此，在 Substance Painter 或 ZBrush 中，你要将高多边形网格缩小 automated process. So in Substance Painter or ZBrush you'll take your high polygon mesh reduce 然后使用一个名为 "烘焙 "的过程来创建这些法线贴图，并将其作为 the poly count and then use a process called baking to create these normal maps and take the 细节，但将其转换为法线贴图，这样我们就可以将其用于有 detail in the high poly mesh but convert it into this normal map so we can use it on something with 大大减少了多边形。因此，Nanite 无疑改变了游戏规则，使虚幻 significantly less polygons. So Nanite has definitely changed the game and allowed Unreal 引擎可以使用更多的多边形，几乎到了对多边形数量不加限制的地步 Engine to use a lot more polygons almost to the point where there is no restriction on the amount 的多边形。但如果要对地表进行建模，这就需要一个疯狂的 of polygons that we have. But if you're going to model the surface this would require an insane 这里的每一个小角落和缝隙都有大量的几何图形。很多时候 amount of geometry for every little nook and crevice that's existing on here. A lot of times 对我们来说，在每个像素而非 it is going to be easier for us to have this micro detail happening at a per pixel basis instead of a 每个多边形。因此，尽可能使用法线贴图仍然是个好主意。我们可以从 per polygon basis. So it's still a good idea to use normal maps wherever you can. We can start 来增加我们的正常强度。我们还可以将其设置为负值 to increase our normal strength. Another thing we can do is we can also set it to a negative value 然后把它反转过来，让这些看起来像有缝隙的地方朝向 and invert it and instead of having these look like there's crevices that are going in towards 在这个物体的中心，我们就会看到这些向外延伸的凸起。因此有一个直接的 the center of this object we'll start to have these bumps going outward. So there is a direct 这里的关系和法线受到场景中灯光的影响。您可以看到灯光 relationship here and normals are affected by the lights in the scene. You can see that the light 如果我把它移到左侧这里 starts to catch on the sides here based on where that light is and if I move it to the left here 你会发现，这些凸起的左侧好像在迎光。 you'll see that it looks like the left side of these bumps are catching the light.
evidences.speaker: instructor
